methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $246M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
FuriosaAI is a Series C semiconductor chip company that has raised $246M to date. Valuation analysis triangulates across a universe of 8 private peers (including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX) and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), reflecting the frontier-tech hardware landscape.

Two methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $418M. Peer-median methodology anchors to four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies with a median valuation of $1.9B (range: $529M to $5.0B). Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 20.6x across public semiconductors) is not applicable due to absence of revenue disclosure. These two methods produce a triangulated range of $355M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B.

The wide dispersion—spanning nearly 6x from low to high—reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage semiconductor valuations. Stage-progression alone suggests $418M; peer comps suggest $1.9B. Key drivers of ultimate valuation within this range include product commercialization trajectory, addressable market capture, and burn rate relative to runway. The peer set itself shows significant variance ($529M to $5B), indicating that company-specific execution risk and market positioning remain dominant value drivers at this stage.
